locations.name: Athlone Town Centre
locations.description_template: The bustling centre of Athlone, a garrison town straddling the Shannon. Soldiers in red coats mix with market traders. The air carries river damp and the smell of baking bread. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: the long road north to Roscommon, following the coach route
the steep lane up to the castle
a short walk down to the Shannon bridge

locations.name: Athlone Castle
locations.description_template: The great Norman castle overlooking the Shannon. Its round keep dominates the skyline. A British garrison occupies the castle in 1820. Sentries stand at the gate. It is {time}. The {weather} sky hangs heavy above the battlements.

connections.path_description: the steep lane back down to the town centre

locations.mythological_significance: The castle guards the crossing of the Shannon since 1210. During the Siege of Athlone in 1691, it withstood a terrible bombardment.

locations.name: Shannon Bridge
locations.description_template: The old stone bridge spanning the Shannon, connecting the east and west banks of Athlone. The river flows dark and steady beneath. Fishermen cast lines from the arches. It is {time}. Weather: {weather}.

connections.path_description: back up from the bridge to the town centre
across the bridge and along the west bank
the long Dublin road, a full day's journey east by coach

locations.mythological_significance: The Shannon — Sionann, named for the goddess who drowned seeking the salmon of knowledge.

locations.name: Luan Gallery
locations.description_template: A converted warehouse on the west bank of the Shannon, used for parish meetings and occasional exhibitions. The stone walls keep it cool in summer and freezing in winter. It is {time}. Outside: {weather}.

connections.path_description: back along the west bank to the bridge
a riverside walk to the coach station

locations.name: Athlone Coach Station
locations.description_template: The busy coach station where mail coaches depart for Dublin, Galway, and Roscommon. Horses stamp in the yard. A timekeeper calls out departures. It is {time}. The sky is {weather}.

connections.path_description: a riverside walk back to the gallery
